recommendedArchitecture: Measurement-anchored surgical evolution of the existing conforming mesher â€” keep the registry/quadtree watertight topology skeleton untouched and rebuild only the METRIC layer on top of it. Base = Design A ("Surgical evolution"), with Design C's single-source warp-composed PullbackMetric + Klincsek-DP commitment + certified-exception validator semantics, and Design B's metric-stretched per-cell CDT and composed-sampler (d'Azevedo) lattice alignment for SpiralRidges, all nine critiques' required amendments applied. Concretely: (0) commit an authoritative baseline matrix and land every instrument (fold/drop counters, triangle provenance, sliver attribution, ceiling map, TRUE-instrument B-sweep) before any behavior change; (1) certify the dead shaped-template path (Klincsek O(n^3) max-min-3D-angle DP replacing the greedy ear-clip at QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:149-191) and arm it with a warp-composed pullback metric (leaf.efg populated in leaves() only â€” verified dead today, declared-never-assigned at PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:65), mirrored into the CDT path's hardcoded plain-cell branch in the same re-baseline epoch; (2) pre-authorized GATE-B percentile damping for the connectivity-immune thin-cell sliver class; (3) root-fix the constrained-CDT fill (fail-loud on BOTH masking channels at ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:85-87, PASS-B re-planarization extended to T-contacts/overlaps, unconditional edge-local merge rules with feature-line provenance, optional FP-safe metric-stretched cdt2d) â€” this stage also owns the CelticKnot short-wide bnd=6 crack; (4) a residual-gated crest-band escalation ladder (registration-time edge-local in-metric snap â†’ frozen-metric interior Lawson flips â†’ accept-only-if-3D-improves crest-aligned Steiners via the proven replay wiring â†’ ribbons only as documented last escalation); (5) SpiralRidges decided by the measured per-cell corner-angle ceiling map: close as no-op, or composed-sampler lattice alignment (positions arithmetically identical), or a certified ceiling-bound exception; (6) extremes matrix + two-tier CI (per-PR CPU goal-vector gates, nightly pinned-real-GPU full matrix) + validator semantics that admit certified exceptions, handing the green matrix to the existing cutover plan (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-export-pipeline-cutover-plan.md) â€” whose mesher-independent Phase-2 wiring starts in parallel immediately, with an interim default-flip checkpoint on a DOMINANCE criterion (conforming >= legacy on every counter) after Stage 3 rather than perfection. Every behavior-changing stage ships behind a __pf dev lever (matching the existing __pfConforming* idiom), re-baselines are batched into exactly three epochs (S1+S2, S3, S4) plus the style-scoped S5, and the 20-style hash baseline is re-archived at the end of every epoch so the byte-identity tripwire is never dark. Hard constraints absolute throughout: every change is interior-only connectivity, refinement-decision-only, or an edge-local interior-independent point function; no post-hoc repair/weld/T-junction pass anywhere; u stays periodic; vertices stay exact analytic GPU evaluations of (u,t).
rationale: Three independent designs converged on the same diagnosis, and my code spot-checks confirm it: the topology skeleton (periodic 2:1-balanced quadtree + grid-line vertex registry + per-cell constrained CDT + analytic GPU evaluation) is literature-standard and sound â€” bnd=0/nonMan~0 everywhere â€” while every open defect (9-style slivers, helix shear, crest serration, SFB@1 nonMan, high-relief residuals) is a metric/robustness error in the triangulation CHOICES made blind to the true 3D metric. The research survey is unanimous that the fix is in-metric (first-fundamental-form, warp-composed by Jacobian pullback) connectivity selection â€” and the codebase already contains the 80% version as verified dead code: I confirmed shapedTemplate/earClipMaxMinAngle exist and are gated on leaf.efg which production never assigns (single declaration at PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts:65, no writes). So rip-and-replace is strictly dominated, and the real decision was between the three stagings. I base on Design A because its critique profile required only amendments, never structural retraction: it alone correctly refused both the plain-efg handoff error AND the discredited-instrument justifications, and its stages are each interior-only or observability-only under the registry contract. Design B's centerpiece (ribbons) failed the empirical lens as a committed stage â€” it funds the largest, most novel work against a residual that is unmeasured at decision time and plausibly already near CAD tolerance (TRUE-metric SFB crestRms ~0.08-0.13mm), while cheaper competitors (metric-stretched CDT, registration-time snapping, flips, corrected-kernel Steiners) go unmeasured; I demote ribbons to a documented, residual-gated escalation with B's own de-scope ladder. Design C's framing ("refoundation") is right about coherence â€” I adopt its PullbackMetric single-source module, Klincsek commitment, ceiling-map instrument, percentile-B lever, and certified-exception semantics â€” but its tensor-sizing stage maps to no measured defect (per-axis targets cannot change cell aspect under square splits + one global B per wall pair, verified at WatertightAssembly.ts:110-150) and its gates repeatedly staked hard zeros on unmeasured attributions. The decisive sequencing corrections come from the critiques and are now baked in: (1) no gate may reference a baseline that does not exist â€” e2e/baselines/ holds only default-dims triangle-quality probes, so the authoritative 20x5 matrix is Stage 0, not Stage 7; (2) every instrument lands before the behavior change it gates (the two masking channels in ConstrainedCellTriangulator â€” silent negative-area flip AND silent area==0 drop, both verified at :82-87 â€” must both be counted, or topology gates certify the wrong invariant); (3) attribution before hard gates â€” among the 9 slivered styles only Voronoi routes through the CDT path (verified in the EXTRACTORS table, FeatureLineGraph.ts:735-782: BasketWeave/CelticTriquetra emit only axis-aligned/rim lines, Crystalline/ArtDeco honest-empty), and thin-cell slivers are provably connectivity-immune, so gates are written per fix-class with the percentile-B contingency pre-authorized instead of deadlocking; (4) the B=3-vs-B=2 serration trade is re-decided on TRUE instruments because the -38% figure was measured with the reference-dominated metric the project itself discredited; (5) cross-cell symmetry of shared-edge merges is inviolable â€” every proposed per-cell-conditioned boundary merge from all three designs is rejected and replaced by unconditional, registry-provenance-based, edge-local rules; (6) user value is front-loaded: measured temporary containment can ship at Stage 0, mesher-independent cutover wiring runs in parallel, and the default flip proceeds on dominance over the (currently defective) legacy path after Stage 3 rather than on perfection â€” crest serration is a quality follow-up behind the flag, not a cutover hostage. Meshing and integration are therefore INTERLEAVED, not meshing-first: the cutover plan's plumbing has no meshing dependency and the throwing conforming validator only becomes safe once Stage 1-3 zeros land, which is exactly the dominance checkpoint.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 0 â€” Ground truth + instruments + plan reconciliation (production byte-identical; ~1 week; STARTS IMMEDIATELY)
stagedPlan.what: (a) Run and COMMIT the authoritative baseline the cutover plan's Task 0.1/0.2 specifies: 20 styles x {default, short-wide H40/R150, tall-narrow, high-flare, no-drain} plus adversarial style params (SFB sf_strength=1, BasketWeave twist!=0) at production opts on the faithful real-WebGPU e2e path, recording sliver(aspect>100)/bnd/nonMan/orient/featDrop, triangleQualityDistribution, crestBandTriangleQuality (with a repeated-run variance baseline to pre-register epsilon), wallChordError with the decoupled __pfReferenceDenseRes reference, build wall-clock (n>=3), and per-style default-dims export hashes. (b) Land dev-gated observability: pre-flip negative-area counter AND silent area==0 drop counter in ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:82-87 with a one-cell dump/replay harness and a triangle->emitting-cell-key map so confirmation is causal (cell-set intersection with diagnoseTopoQuality defects), not correlational; per-triangle provenance channel (plain-quad diagonal / transition fan / FCT plain cell / FCT feature cell / wall surfaceId); per-style sliver attribution bucketing thin-cell (cellAspect3D-bound, connectivity-immune) vs bad-template; SpiralRidges per-cell corner-angle ceiling map (general F-inclusive form, not the F=0 shortcut). (c) TRUE-instrument forced-B sweep {0,1,2,3,auto} via the existing __pfConformingUBias lever (WatertightAssembly.ts:371-374) across the 9 slivered styles + SFB@1 B2-vs-B3 measured with crestBandTriangleQuality + decoupled crestRms â€” the -38% GATE-B claim is inadmissible (discredited instrument). If B=3 shows no true serration gain over B=2, ship the one-line hasFeatures B<=2 cap immediately as documented TEMPORARY containment (removes the shipping SFB nonMan and the worst CDT-style slivers while the program runs; lifted at Stage 3's gate). (d) Reconcile the two plans in writing: this design supersedes cutover Tasks 1.2 (GATE-A un-defer: stays deferred, wash-measured) and 1.3 (rotated cells: rejected, ArtDeco-cascade rationale); mesher-independent cutover Phase-2 wiring (flag source, validationSummary unification, format, memory guard) starts in parallel NOW; per-PR CPU evalSurface goal-vector CI gates (plan Task 3.1 pattern) land as soon as the baseline exists.
stagedPlan.gate: Authoritative matrix + per-style hashes committed to e2e/baselines/; counters, attribution table, ceiling map, and B-sweep table recorded for all 20 styles at default; SFB@1 repro harness reproduces (or refutes) the fold-over signature causally; production exports hash-identical to pre-stage (all instruments dev-gated); 318+ unit tests green. Decision artifacts in hand: per-style fix-class attribution and the B=3-vs-B=2 verdict.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 1 â€” Certify then arm: Klincsek DP templates + warp-composed pullback metric, mirrored into the CDT plain branch (re-baseline epoch 1; ~1.5-2.5 weeks)
stagedPlan.what: (a) Replace the greedy ear loop in earClipMaxMinAngle (QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:149-191 â€” verified: unguarded break at :181 can strand boundary sub-edges, final-3 emission at :188-190 has no area check) with Klincsek's O(n^3) max-min-3D-angle dynamic program: zero-area triangles scored -Infinity, complete triangulation guaranteed (k-2 positive-area triangles, every boundary sub-edge covered exactly once â€” both weak-convexity crack hazards closed BY CONSTRUCTION). Commit to the DP; no greedy-patch alternative. Exhaustive property test (rectangle+mids polygons incl. collinear corner-mid-corner runs and feature-path k>8 polygons, x anisotropic/sheared metrics to 16:1, >=1e5 cases) asserting coverage/orientation/count invariants. Atomic per-cell emission with all-or-nothing rollback to the legacy centroid fan. (b) New PullbackMetric module: closed-form Jacobian composition of u-warp/t-warp/helix (E'=E*phi'^2, F'=phi'(F-sE), G'=G-2sF+s^2E) mirroring the EXACT application guards at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2418-2481 (helix only when u-warp identity; t-warp walls-only; helix both walls; caps rigid), pinned per style class by finite-difference-vs-closed-form unit tests; gridResolution() forwarded so metricStepsForSampler never falls back to DEFAULT_H=1e-4; GpuSurfaceSampler unfolded-u wrap verified BEFORE composing; assertion that warp-anchor kinks lie on cell boundaries (or a defined one-sided phi' convention). (c) Populate leaf.efg lazily in leaves() ONLY (never leafOfCell â€” hot neighbors() path), retained metric sampler threaded per-wall through ConformingWallOptions -> buildConformingWall -> buildQuadtreeAtScale and AssemblyWallOptions (BOTH walls; inner walls of CDT styles share the outer uBias). Warp-composed from day one â€” plain efg never ships alone (u-/t-warps are also post-triangulation remaps; plain efg is wrong exactly at the pinned creases of most slivered styles). (d) In the SAME epoch, mirror the shaped templates into FeatureConformingTriangulator's hardcoded plain-cell branch (:687-707): metric shorter-diagonal for 0-split cells, certified DP for transition cells. Sub-flagged separately (__pfConformingEfg, __pfConformingShapedTemplates, __pfConformingShapedCdtCells) so any matrix regression bisects metric-vs-template-vs-port. Per-leaf instrumentation of which leaves fired shaped templates (makes the B=0 byte-identity clause mechanically checkable). Re-verify the TRIS_PER_LEAF=2 budget-search calibration (ConformingWall.ts:181) after the transition-cell triangle-count drop.
stagedPlan.gate: Property suite green (0 cracks, 0 zero-area emissions). Per the Stage-0 attribution: bad-template-class slivers -> 0 PER STYLE on ArtDeco(2107)/Crystalline(2027)/DragonScales(2683)/GeometricStar(166)/BambooSegments(291)/BasketWeave(1584)/CelticTriquetra(1221) (all plain-path per verified routing) + the plain cells/inner walls of CDT styles, attributed via the provenance channel. SpiralRidges gets its OWN sub-gate (count + ceiling-map verdict â€” NOT a hard ->0; pre-registered decision rule routes to Stage 5). bnd=0/orient=0/nonMan unchanged-or-better 20/20 at default. wallChordError (decoupled ref) within the pre-registered +/- band on smooth styles (the omitted centroid removes an on-surface sample per transition cell â€” this gate is mandatory). B=0 clean styles byte-identical OR every diff enumerated by the leaf-level instrument as a >2:1-aspect ANISO_ASPECT_GATE crossing. Build <= +10% vs Stage-0 baseline at the 'high' profile (measured â€” the '<1% efg cost' claim is not assumed). Full re-measure protocol once; hashes re-archived.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 2 â€” GATE-B percentile damping + gradation (pre-authorized contingency; lands inside epoch 1 if attribution demands; ~2-5 days)
stagedPlan.what: Fires only if Stage-0/1 attribution shows thin-cell-class slivers remain â€” cells GATE B physically stretched to 2^B:1 in 3D, which NO triangulation can fix (the documented ArtDeco B=3 mechanism, WatertightAssembly.ts:42-49). Recalibrate computeUBias (WatertightAssembly.ts:110-151) from the single worst-point maxURatio to a percentile of the u-dominant ratio over the 192x192 lattice, with a relief-gated floor. Pre-registered candidate set {P90, P95, worst-point} x floor values, time-boxed, with the Stage-0 TRUE B-sweep as the serration-vs-sliver tiebreaker: if high B buys no true-instrument serration gain on a conflicted style, B drops (this dissolves Design C's pre-committed deadlock). One B per wall pair stays (ring-match invariant); biasFreeU/buildCreaseRefineLines crease coverage pinned by test BEFORE the change (6aafe5b regression sentinel). Optional log-ratio gradation bound in MetricSizingField only if grading-transition slivers are measured at the extremes probe (run short-wide on ArtDeco/CelticKnot here, early, so a late global sizing change cannot invalidate everything at Stage 6). Design C's full directional tensor sizing is explicitly NOT built. Sizing-decision-only: vertex derivation rules untouched, watertight by the same four invariants.
stagedPlan.gate: sliver=0 (aspect>100, summarizeConformingValidation) on all 8 plain-path slivered styles at default dims; SFB@1 + Gothic crest serration non-regression on TRUE instruments (crestBandTriangleQuality + decoupled crestRms); Gothic crease coverage >=0.9 (diagnoseFeatures); per-style leaf-count drift within a stated band at fixed budget; smooth-style chord error unchanged within band; time-box expiry fallback = keep worst-point B + templates + documented residual.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 3 â€” CDT constraint-topology hardening at root: SFB@1 nonMan, Voronoi, CelticKnot short-wide crack (re-baseline epoch 2; ~1.5-2.5 weeks)
stagedPlan.what: Blocked on Stage-0's causal repro (signature appears/disappears under a merge-disable or re-planarize toggle in the dumped cell). EXPLICIT refutation branch: if pre-flip inversions AND zero-area drops are 0 at B=3, the planarize-before-merge hypothesis is falsified â€” reopen the dedupSide/interior-weld/resolve asymmetry investigation (FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts:743-813) with a fresh minimal repro before ANY fix lands; B<=2 containment stays meanwhile. Fixes, with all watertight amendments baked in: (i) PASS-B re-planarization over the RESOLVED index-space constraints (between :814 and :822) extended beyond proper crossings to vertex-on-segment T-contacts (reuse the existing vertex â€” no new point) and collinear-overlapping constraint pairs, with deterministic handling of crossings inside the 1e-7 eps band; unit pin: zero interior crossings AND zero vertex-on-interior contacts before cdt2d. (ii) Every planarize Steiner (PASS A and B) gets clearance to all four sides AND to every existing cell vertex and constraint segment (>=STEINER_MIN_EDGE_DIST=2e-6, >2x WELD_TAU, >QSCALE quantum); on violation, a deterministic INTERIOR-ONLY terminal degradation (drop the shorter crossing chord in this cell, counted, surfaced in featureDrop) with a termination argument for the planarize-to-fixpoint loop. (iii) Fail-loud on BOTH masking channels (negative-area flips AND area==0 drops â€” (u,t)-collinear is not 3D-collinear, so a drop is a potential hole) with a per-cell completeness assertion (boundary sub-edges 1-incident, interior edges 2-incident). (iv) resolve() tie-break pinned to deterministic min-qk. (v) Cross-feature-line merge restrictions made UNCONDITIONAL and edge-local by threading feature-line provenance into the registry (Seg -> CellPoint -> SidePoint; today collectSegments destroys identity and regAdd is first-write-wins) â€” NEVER condition a side-point merge on per-cell planarity or inversion state; fallback retries restricted to interior-only moves; a both-neighbours symmetric-side test asserts identical shared-edge vertex sets with fallback on/off. (vi) Optional, sub-flagged, same epoch: per-cell metric-stretched cdt2d with FP-safety amendments â€” per-axis power-of-two stretch factors (exponent-only, exact collinearity preserved) or the metric applied inside the in-circle predicate; orientation/degeneracy classification pinned to RAW (u,t); boundary-pair distinctness + along-edge order guard at QSCALE resolution with deterministic fallback to unstretched; condition-number gate on M^(1/2); property test over exactly-collinear on-edge mids. (vii) Diagnose+fix the CelticKnot short-wide bnd=6 forced-crossing crack HERE (same file/machinery; adopt the cutover plan Task 1.1 repro recipe; the fix must restore bnd=0 within the registry contract â€” no patch passes). Containment cap lifted when this gate goes green.
stagedPlan.gate: SFB@1 at auto-B: nonMan 3->0, duplicated edges gone; Voronoi nonMan 1->0 and slivers 2076 -> 0 or ceiling-certified residual; inversion + drop counters == 0 on all 20 styles at DEFAULT dims (matrix-wide extremes counter gate deferred to Stage 6); featureDrop(real)=0 AND fallback-activation counter == 0 at default on Hive/CelticKnot/Gyroid/Voronoi/SFB; CelticKnot short-wide bnd 6->0; crestBandTriangleQuality non-regression on all inserted-feature styles; all gates on real e2e exports; re-baseline of inserted-feature styles once; hashes re-archived.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 4 â€” Crest-band quality floor: residual-gated escalation ladder for diagonal/helical crest serration (re-baseline epoch 3; ~1-3 weeks depending on residual)
stagedPlan.what: DECISION GATE FIRST (the pattern Designs A/B applied to their conditional stages but B failed to apply to its centerpiece): re-measure crestBandTriangleQuality (numeric: bandPctBelow15 <= bulkPctBelow15 + epsilon AND bulk not regressed vs Stage-0 baseline, epsilon pre-registered from the variance baseline) + min-angle tint + TRUE chord error on SFB@1/Hive/CelticKnot/Gyroid/Voronoi AFTER Stages 1-3. Fund only what the residual demands, in cost order: (a) Registration-time edge-local in-metric clearance snap: crest/constraint edge-points within metric-epsilon of a registry grid vertex snap ONTO it via a rule that is a pure function of (global curve, grid line), with the metric evaluated AT the candidate edge point (never a cell-center efg), candidates processed in canonical sorted order with min-qk ties; registry vertices never move. One unified rule replacing per-stage merge tweaks â€” and the ONLY mechanism that can remove the tol=1e-9 transition-edge dedup-survivor needles, whose short edge is a boundary sub-edge that interior flips provably cannot eliminate. (b) Frozen-per-cell-metric Lawson flips of interior, non-constraint, non-boundary edges (constant metric = affine pullback => termination + constrained max-min-metric-angle optimality), preceded by the per-cell completeness assertion (skip+count incomplete complexes), with 3D-verified acceptance on the targeted crest cells so frozen-metric flips cannot locally regress them. (c) Crest-aligned interior Steiners via the PROVEN refineCellInterior replay wiring (FCT:836-842) with a metric-true kernel replacing the measured-harmful flat-affine one: tangent read from data.constraints chords, candidates offset along the metric normal at metric-unit spacing in (u,t), positions evaluated on the true surface, each insertion accepted ONLY if the measured 3D min angle improves (Bossen-Heckbert) â€” worst case is a no-op, never a manufactured sliver; clearance extended to ALL existing cell vertices and constraint segments, not just sides; CPU-side ruler adequacy near crests verified first, else batch-insert + post-hoc GPU-evaluated measurement. (d) Offset-curve ribbons remain a DOCUMENTED ESCALATION ONLY, never default-funded: pre-written de-scope ladder (per-style enablement keyed on measured residual; delta FROZEN from the pre-insertion sizing field to kill the belowFeatureFloor refinement feedback; per-axis RAW-(u,t) clearance floors vs cornerSnapU/anchor boxes/WELD_TAU/QSCALE; junction/taper trimming with cap rungs ending on shared crest vertices; FCT:625 sub-snap piece-drop handling; featureLevel/cornerSnap frozen to pre-ribbon values; spatial index for clearance queries; budget/TRIS_PER_LEAF recalibration in the gate). Expected outcome: unfunded. Also measure the non-inserted diagonal-relief mass (CelticTriquetra braid bands, twisted BasketWeave â€” deliberately not emitted per the EXTRACTORS table) and either document it as an out-of-scope sizing-only residual or scope extraction extension separately â€” the serration claim is scoped honestly.
stagedPlan.gate: crestBandTriangleQuality numeric parity (band <= bulk + epsilon, bulk non-regressed) on SFB@1/Hive/CelticKnot/Gyroid/Voronoi; zero red clusters on crests in the e2e/_crest_stage0.cjs tint render (archived); per-style GLOBAL triangleQualityDistribution and sliver counts not worsened (anti-relocation check â€” needles may not be pushed just outside the band); Voronoi residual slivers -> 0; featureDrop(real)=0 maintained; feature-dense build <= 1.5x Stage-0 baseline; non-inserted-relief residual quantified and dispositioned.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 5 â€” SpiralRidges resolution: ceiling-map decision -> no-op, composed-sampler lattice alignment, or certified floor (style-scoped; 0-1.5 weeks)
stagedPlan.what: Decision rule pre-registered at Stage 0: if post-Stage-1/2 SpiralRidges sliver=0 AND crest band ~ bulk, CLOSE as a no-op. If the measured residual clusters AT the per-cell analytic ceiling (corner angle of the post-warp parallelogram â€” aspect-independent, so uBias provably cannot help) and exceeds the crest gate: composed-sampler lattice alignment (d'Azevedo): build the SpiralRidges quadtree in sheared coordinates v = u - s*t via a ComposedWarpSampler used for sizing + metric + final evaluation â€” final positions arithmetically identical to today's post-triangulation applyHelixWarp, but cells become 3D rectangles and crest columns stay lattice-vertical, so the existing crease pin/refine machinery applies unchanged. Amendments baked in (from B's watertight critique): caps/rim interior vertices remapped with the SAME composed map (keep applyHelixWarp's cap branch as a position-identical pass) with cap-triangle orientation and degeneracy checks in the gate; structural exclusivity assertion between the sheared lattice and the post-warp (assembly asserts the other is identity â€” no double shear); chooseHelixGrid dyadic-anchor refusal and buildCreaseRefineLines source resolution re-derived in (v,t); per-seam-column boundary-edge count in the gate (helix crosses the seam 'turns' times). Watertightness restated, not replaced: sheared grid lines are global analytic curves evaluated identically by both neighbours; shear commutes with u->u+1. Design A's alternative (re-route helical crests as inserted general curves) is rejected â€” see rejectedIdeas. If lattice alignment is declined on cost: accept a QUANTIFIED ceiling-bound floor, committed as a certified per-style exception in the Stage-6 validator semantics â€” never an uncounted defect, and flagged to the user as a product decision.
stagedPlan.gate: SpiralRidges sliver 479 -> 0 + crest tint clean + TRUE chord <= 0.03mm + featureDrop=0 + bnd/nonMan/orient=0 incl. per-seam-column counts, OR every residual sub-15-degree triangle certified within tolerance of its cell's analytic ceiling with the exception list committed; ALL other styles hash-identical (the change is style-scoped); single-style re-baseline executed.

stagedPlan.stage: Stage 6 â€” Dimension-extremes hardening, two-tier CI, validator semantics, cutover handshake (~1-1.5 weeks active + matrix runtime)
stagedPlan.what: (a) Full DoD matrix: 20 styles x 5 dimension sets PLUS the adversarial style params, on the faithful real-WebGPU e2e path; inversion/drop/fallback counters == 0 matrix-wide; the short-wide budget/sliver-explosion class (1.5-2.9M slivers, ArtDeco timeout, MAX_BUDGET_SCALE=4 soft cap) owned here with the MetricSizingField gradation bound as the named scoped lever â€” already probed at Stage 2 on the worst styles so it cannot invalidate all baselines late. (b) CI two-tier: per-PR headless CPU evalSurface goal-vector gates (cutover plan Task 3.1 pattern â€” landed back at Stage 0/1, ratified here); nightly full matrix on a pinned real-GPU machine (Windows/WebGPU box documented as the reference); two-tier hashing â€” machine-independent CPU (u,t)-topology/connectivity hash, float-position hashes pinned per (GPU, driver); determinism measured by duplicate runs BEFORE the gate is trusted; red/green demonstrated by injecting a known-bad commit. Per-PR real-WebGPU gating is explicitly not attempted (no runner; SwiftShader breaks position expectations). (c) Validator semantics resolved (this dissolves Design C's Stage-5/6 contradiction): conforming validity = zero-vector OR a certified, per-style, committed ceiling-bound exception list, with unified advisory/fatal semantics in summarizeConformingValidation + useParametricExport â€” an accepted floor can never permanently block cutover. (d) Hand the green matrix to the cutover plan's flag-flip phase. The INTERIM DOMINANCE CHECKPOINT (executable any time after Stage 3): if the authoritative matrix shows conforming >= legacy on every counter for every style/dim, the default flip proceeds THEN with crest-band work continuing behind the default-on flag â€” crest serration is not part of the validity gate and must not hold user value hostage; legacy today ships 9 styles with hundreds-to-tens-of-thousands of orientation mismatches.
stagedPlan.gate: The Definition-of-Done vector, measured not claimed: every style x every dimension set reports sliver=0, boundaryEdges=0, nonManifold=0, orientationMismatches=0, featuresDropped=0 (or carries a committed certified exception); crest-band numeric parity + archived clean tint renders; TRUE chord error <= 0.03mm on smooth regions at 'high'; 318+ tests green; default build within 1.5x of the Stage-0 envelope, feature-dense <= 1.5x; CI red/green and determinism demonstrated; matrix green twice consecutively; cutover handoff executed with the post-program hash baseline re-archived. Realistic program total: ~8-13 weeks solo including matrix runtime, with the dominance checkpoint as the named early-exit after Stage 3.

adoptedFromRunnersUp: From Design C (Metric-Native Refoundation): the PullbackMetric single-source module with closed-form Jacobian composition pinned per style class by finite-difference-vs-closed-form unit tests; the firm commitment to Klincsek DP (Design A waffled with a greedy-patch alternative its own critique showed weaker); the per-cell analytic corner-angle ceiling instrument â€” generalized to the F-inclusive form and MOVED to Stage 0 so ceiling-vs-mischoice is decidable before any gate is judged; percentile-damped GATE B with relief floor as the pre-authorized thin-cell contingency; the certified-exception validator semantics that let an accepted quality floor coexist with a hard cutover gate; two-tier CI hashing (CPU topology hash machine-independent, float hashes per GPU/driver).

From Design B (Crest-Structured Ribbons): per-cell metric-stretched cdt2d as the cheap feature-cell fill upgrade â€” adopted with the FP-safety amendments (power-of-two stretch or metric-in-predicate, raw-space orientation checks, boundary-distinctness guard with deterministic unstretched fallback) and sequenced BEFORE any ribbon talk; the composed-sampler (d'Azevedo) lattice alignment for SpiralRidges (B Stage 7 / C Stage 5ii), preferred over Design A's general-curve re-route, with the cap-remap and exclusivity amendments; the snap-at-registration unification of crest-point dedup (one edge-local rule replacing three conflicting per-stage merge tweaks); the full ribbon spec + de-scope ladder retained verbatim as the documented last-resort escalation so future sessions need not re-derive it; the identity-warp byte-identity discipline (subsumed by going warp-composed day one with per-style hash checks).

From the nine critiques (now binding parts of the plan): Stage 0 authoritative baseline + ALL instruments before any behavior change; both masking channels (flips AND zero-area drops) counted, with causal not correlational confirmation; sliver attribution (thin-cell vs bad-template) before hard sliver->0 gates; the TRUE-instrument B-sweep replacing the discredited -38% justification; re-baseline batching into three epochs with per-epoch hash re-archival; __pf kill-switch levers on every behavior-changing stage; CelticKnot short-wide crack ownership in the CDT stage; the interim dominance-based cutover checkpoint; numeric pre-registered thresholds replacing every '~bulk'/'measurably' comparator; the per-triangle provenance channel that makes per-stage attribution falsifiable; SpiralRidges carved out of any multi-style AND-gate.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Ribbons (offset-curve crest ladders + rungs) as the committed centerpiece (Design B Stage 5 as written)
rejectedIdeas.why: The only large-effort stage in any design NOT gated on a measured residual â€” funded against a defect whose magnitude at decision time is unknown and plausibly already near CAD tolerance (TRUE-metric SFB@1 crestRms ~0.08-0.13mm). Known tar pits: offset cusps/self-intersection, junction collisions on braid styles, the delta floor specified in metric space while every snap/weld hazard is per-axis raw (u,t) (cornerSnapU=cornerSnap/2^B verified at FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts:263-264), taper-to-zero driving offset vertices inside WELD_TAU of crest vertices by construction, the FCT:625 sub-snap piece-drop gapping offsets per cell, and synthetic lines feeding featureLevel->cornerSnap (a hidden global re-baseline) and belowFeatureFloor (forced refinement + budget coarsening of the bulk). Cheaper competitors (stretched CDT, registration snap, flips, corrected-kernel Steiners) must measurably fail first. Retained ONLY as the Stage-4(d) documented escalation with B's de-scope ladder.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Greedy ear-clip 'minimal diff' hardening as an alternative to Klincsek DP (Design A Stage 1 option)
rejectedIdeas.why: Under-specified to the point of being a different, weaker design: no admissible-ear existence proof at each reduction step, undefined behavior if the collinear-remainder filter rejects every ear, and hand-waved final-3 backtracking. The DP delivers the certified complete-triangulation guarantee (every boundary sub-edge covered exactly once â€” the actual crack hazard at QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:181/188-190, verified) at negligible O(n^3) cost for n<=12. Greedy-guard work is throwaway once the DP ships.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Conditioning dedupSide/side-point merges on per-cell planarity, or inversion-triggered retries that re-run boundary merges (variants in all three designs' CDT stages)
rejectedIdeas.why: Breaks the cross-cell symmetry that IS the T-junction-free guarantee: dedupSide is watertight only because both neighbours run an identical unconditional procedure over identical registry input; any interior-dependent condition lets the two cells derive different shared-edge vertex sets â€” a crack BY CONSTRUCTION. Also undecidable today: registry CellPoints carry no feature-line provenance (regAdd is first-write-wins). Replaced by unconditional, edge-local rules with provenance threaded through the registry, and fallbacks restricted to interior-only moves with a both-neighbours symmetry test.

rejectedIdeas.idea: The hasFeatures B<=2 cap as the FIX for the SFB@1 non-manifold â€” and equally, the categorical 'never cap' stance
rejectedIdeas.why: Both sides argued from bad evidence. The cap-as-fix leaves the planarize-before-merge bug armed for any future high-B style and forfeits quality IF B=3 has real value; but 'never cap because of the proven -38% serration gain' cites a measurement made with the reference-dominated metric the project itself discredited (~2.6x inflated; TRUE crestRms already ~CAD tolerance). No TRUE-instrument B2-vs-B3 comparison exists. Resolution: the Stage-0 TRUE-instrument B-sweep decides; the cap is permitted only as explicitly temporary, documented containment, lifted at Stage 3's gate.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Re-routing SpiralRidges helical crests as inserted general-curve features (Design A Stage 6b)
rejectedIdeas.why: As specified it guarantees wall-cap ring cracks: helicalLine emits endpoints at exactly t=0/t=1 and registerBoundary has no global boundary-row exclusion, so every helix endpoint subdivides a pinned ring that must conform to the caps' fixed nRing vertex set. It also loads the CDT path with k constraints crossing nearly every cell (the heaviest machinery, made load-bearing for one style) and surrenders the warp's exact column pinning. The composed-sampler lattice alignment achieves crest alignment with positions arithmetically identical to today and zero new insertion load â€” strictly dominant.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Full directional tensor sizing â€” per-node (hU,hT) targets (Design C Stage 3 first half)
rejectedIdeas.why: Cannot change cell ASPECT under square 4-way splits + one global B per wall pair (ring-match invariant) â€” it only changes density, so it maps to NO measured defect in the current ledger; the claimed ArtDeco-history justification is a misattribution (those were transition-template slivers in a clean metric, fixed by Stage 1). Its over-refinement interaction with the leafCount-only budget search risks regressing the shipped 0.021mm facet-free win. Deferred until a measured defect demands it; only the scalar gradation bound is retained as a scoped extremes lever.

rejectedIdeas.idea: refineCellInterior's flat-3D-plane off-center + barycentric back-map kernel; isotropic interior Steiner refinement; raising the 256x256 sampler; sag/budget/maxLevel tightening as a crest fix
rejectedIdeas.why: All measured FALSE by the project (the flat-plane kernel MANUFACTURES slivers on curved surfaces; sampler raising was byte-identical on worst angle; sag/budget refine the bulk, not the crest tail). They stay dead. Only the replay WIRING of refineCellInterior is reused (Stage 4c), with a metric-true kernel and accept-only-if-measured-3D-min-angle-improves so failure degenerates to a no-op.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Per-axis quadtree splitting with 2-axis balance (the uExtra t-analogue / 'rotated cells' of cutover plan Task 1.3)
rejectedIdeas.why: Measured-broken: the eUL cascade caused the documented ArtDeco timeout. Explicitly superseded in the Stage-0 plan reconciliation so no future session re-attempts it without new evidence.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Rip-and-replace pipelines: BLSURF-style curve-first Riemannian advancing front; dual contouring / Manifold DC; OpenSubdiv subdivision + projection; Instant Meshes / QuadriFlow remeshing; LpCVT/ACVT variational remeshing; Labelle-Shewchuk anisotropic Voronoi as an algorithm; GPU fractional tessellation; Sculpt literal pillowing
rejectedIdeas.why: Each forfeits a hard constraint the rebuild exists to guarantee or re-derives what exists: advancing front trades the local by-construction registry guarantee for months of front-collision robustness engineering (kept as the DOCUMENTED FALLBACK with a concrete trigger: crest-band gate unreachable after all four Stage-4 ladder rungs are spent); DC puts QEF vertices off-surface and merges the pot's thin double wall; OpenSubdiv swaps the analytic surface for a limit surface and re-imports crest alignment as cage design; field-aligned remeshers relax vertices off-surface and require the forbidden validate-and-repair pass; LpCVT/ACVT need global vertex freedom; Labelle-Shewchuk is adopted only as the quality-target SPEC (in-metric min angle ~20 deg = what crestBandTriangleQuality measures); fractional tessellation deliberately manufactures transition slivers and its crack rule IS the registry; pillowing is a post-hoc pass. Recorded here so future sessions do not re-litigate.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Per-PR real-WebGPU CI gate (Design A Stage 7 as written) and cross-machine byte-hash CI gating
rejectedIdeas.why: The CI runs typecheck/lint/vitest/build only â€” no GPU runner exists; a SwiftShader fallback breaks position expectations (GPU-evaluated floats differ); GPU float positions are not deterministic across hardware/drivers, so cross-machine hash gates would flake. Replaced by per-PR headless CPU evalSurface goal-vector gates (landing early) + nightly full matrix on a pinned real-GPU machine + two-tier hashing, with determinism measured before the gate is trusted.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Plain-sampler leaf.efg as a standalone first increment (the original handoff's Step A / Design B's identity-gated Stage 3 as a separate landing)
rejectedIdeas.why: The u-warp and t-warp are post-triangulation vertex remaps just like the helix (verified at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2418-2481), so plain efg optimizes the WRONG metric exactly at the pinned creases of DragonScales/GeometricStar/BambooSegments/BasketWeave/CelticTriquetra â€” most of the slivered set â€” and would burn a full re-baseline epoch shipping a known-wrong metric. Warp-composed from day one, with identity warps composing to the base sampler automatically.

rejectedIdeas.idea: 'Drop the newest interior constraint' as a silent CDT fallback (Design B Stage 2)
rejectedIdeas.why: Converts a topology defect into silent crest-fidelity loss (a crest chord stops being a mesh edge) invisible to a topology-only gate â€” the exact 'metric that cannot see the defect' failure mode. Adopted only as a counted, deterministic (global line/segment order), interior-only terminal degradation surfaced in featureDrop, with the fallback-activation counter required to read 0 at default dims.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Hard 'sliver -> 0' multi-style AND-gates staked on connectivity-only changes before attribution (Design B Stages 3/4, Design C Stage 1 '>=80% collective')
rejectedIdeas.why: Thin-cell slivers (cells physically 2^B:1) are provably connectivity-immune â€” no triangulation of a degenerate cell polygon has good angles â€” so such gates can deadlock or be eroded ad hoc on exactly the styles where GATE-B over-correction dominates. Replaced by attribution-derived per-fix-class gates (Stage 0 instrument) with the percentile-B contingency pre-authorized in Stage 2, and SpiralRidges carved into its own ceiling-map sub-gate.

rejectedIdeas.idea: Meshing-first sequencing (all user value after the full meshing program)
rejectedIdeas.why: The delivery critiques are unanimous and correct: legacy ships defectively TODAY (9 styles, orientation mismatches up to ~35k), the conforming validator hard-throws on sliverCount>0 so the flip needs zeros â€” but zeros on the COUNTERS legacy also fails arrive at Stage 3, not Stage 6. Interleaved instead: mesher-independent cutover wiring starts at Stage 0, CPU CI gates land early, measured temporary containment can ship immediately, and the default flip proceeds on the dominance criterion after Stage 3 with crest-band quality continuing behind the default-on flag.

openRisks: Stage-0 attribution may show thin-cell (GATE-B over-correction) slivers dominate several of the 9 styles, making percentile-B load-bearing â€” its serration-vs-sliver calibration is time-boxed with a pre-registered candidate set, but this codebase's history says metric calibration loops are schedule sinkholes; the written fallback (keep worst-point B + templates + documented residual) caps the loss but may leave a nonzero sliver residual on 1-2 styles pending the Stage-6 exception semantics.

The Stage-3 root-cause story (PASS-A planarize before PASS-B merges, masked by the silent flip/drop at ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:82-87) is still a code-reading hypothesis until the Stage-0 causal repro lands; the refutation branch (reopen dedupSide/weld asymmetry) is specified but is a different, possibly larger fix with unbounded diagnosis time â€” B<=2 containment holds the line meanwhile, at SFB@1's 10.4% sub-15-degree quality cost.

Making both masking channels fail-loud may surface latent fold-overs/degenerate-drops on braid-heavy styles that currently pass topology checks by luck â€” by design this is a feature (defects become measurable, triaged as a backlog, not stage exit criteria), but it is a real schedule risk when the counters first run across 20 styles x extremes.

Warp-composition guard drift is a silent quality (not topology) failure mode: a wrapper that mismatches the exact application guards (helix XOR u-warp, t-warp walls-only, both walls, caps) mis-meters the metric precisely in the crest neighbourhoods being fixed; finite-difference pins and per-style crest-band gates are the defense, but a subtle mismatch on one style class could pass bulk gates while quietly under-delivering at crests.

Per-leaf constant cell-center metric on sub-cell relief (the FourierBloom/Crystalline high-relief class) is the same approximation class that killed refineCellInterior â€” bounded here to 'suboptimal diagonal, never corruption' and to no-op Steiners, but this design honestly may NOT zero the high-relief sub-20-degree residual; the named follow-ups (gradation, then per-region anisotropy) are deferred, and the residual would ship as a measured, documented floor.

SpiralRidges may be ceiling-bound below the crest gate AND the composed-sampler lattice alignment may be declined on cost/risk â€” the certified-floor exception path then requires a product-level acceptance from the user (a quantified, localized sub-15-degree population on one style); this decision should be surfaced the moment the Stage-0 ceiling map lands, not at Stage 5.

The short-wide extremes class (1.5-2.9M sliver explosions, ArtDeco timeout, soft MAX_BUDGET_SCALE=4 cap) has a named scoped lever (sizing gradation, probed early) but no proven fix; if the early probe shows the lever insufficient, Stage 6 grows by an unbounded perf-recovery work item and the 5-dims DoD may need a staged definition (default-dims DoD first, extremes DoD second).

Nightly real-GPU CI depends on a single pinned Windows/WebGPU machine â€” a single point of failure for trust in every topology gate; per-(GPU,driver) float-hash pinning also means any driver update invalidates the position-hash tier until re-pinned.

Schedule realism: ~8-13 weeks solo including three re-baseline epochs and matrix runtime (30-60+ min per full pass, multi-hour at feature-dense/high). The dominance checkpoint after Stage 3 is the pressure valve for user value, but it requires the validator-semantics and flag-source wiring (cutover plan Phase 2, running in parallel) to actually be ready when the matrix goes green â€” a cross-track coordination risk.

Process risk under the byte-identity hard constraint: Stages 1-4 each legitimately re-baseline, and the tripwire is only as good as the per-epoch hash re-archival discipline; any 'small' un-flagged change (e.g. the tie-break pinning, which silently changes intra-cell constraint routing) that skips the re-measure protocol violates the constraint unnoticed â€” the per-stage __pf levers and per-epoch hash gates exist precisely to make this auditable, but they must actually be enforced in review.
measurementPlan: Ground truth is established ONCE, before any behavior change, and re-anchored after every re-baseline epoch â€” never assumed. STAGE 0 BASELINE (the single authoritative artifact, committed to e2e/baselines/, which today holds only default-dims triangle-quality probes): 20 styles x {default, short-wide H40/R150, tall-narrow, high-flare, no-drain} + adversarial style params (SFB sf_strength=1, BasketWeave twist!=0), at production opts, through the FAITHFUL e2e real-WebGPU export path (the project's own finding: UV-only unit tests overstate seam cracks â€” no topology claim is ever made from unit tests). Recorded per cell of the matrix: diagnoseTopoQuality (boundaryEdges/nonManifold/orientationMismatches), summarizeConformingValidation sliver count (aspect>100), triangleQualityDistribution (%<15 and %<20 degrees), crestBandTriangleQuality (band AND bulk percentages â€” with a repeated-run variance baseline, n>=3, that pre-registers the epsilon used by every later '~bulk' gate), wallChordError against the DECOUPLED dense reference (__pfReferenceDenseRes â€” the old reference-dominated metric is inadmissible, as is any number derived from it, including the -38% GATE-B serration claim), diagnoseFeatures featureDrop, build wall-clock (n>=3, variance recorded so perf envelopes are testable against noise), and per-style default-dims export hashes. INSTRUMENTS LANDED AT STAGE 0 (all dev-gated, production byte-identical, each validated on known-clean styles before being trusted): the dual fold/drop counters in ConstrainedCellTriangulator with a triangle->emitting-cell-key map enabling CAUSAL confirmation (defect cell-set intersection + appears/disappears under a toggle), the per-triangle provenance channel (plain-quad diagonal / transition fan / FCT plain / FCT feature cell / surfaceId) that makes every per-stage attribution falsifiable, the thin-cell-vs-bad-template sliver attribution bucketing, the SpiralRidges F-inclusive corner-angle ceiling map, and the TRUE-instrument forced-B sweep. PER-STAGE PROTOCOL: every gate's numeric thresholds are written into the stage doc BEFORE work starts (pre-registration â€” the project rejected the 'Round 17 is done' pattern precisely because of post-hoc fuzzy criteria); each re-baseline epoch (S1+S2, S3, S4, style-scoped S5) runs the full 20-style re-measure ONCE at its close: matrix deltas vs the Stage-0 artifact, byte-identity hash checks on styles that must not change (with the per-leaf shaped-template-firing instrument enumerating any permitted B=0 diffs mechanically), snapshot/count-test/TRIS_PER_LEAF budget recalibration, and re-archival of the hash baseline so the byte-identity tripwire is re-armed between epochs. Crest claims are gated on the three faithful instruments together: crestBandTriangleQuality (numeric band-vs-bulk), the e2e/_crest_stage0.cjs min-angle tint render (archived as visual evidence â€” no red on crests), and TRUE chord error on smooth regions (<=0.03mm at 'high'); global distributions are always recorded alongside band metrics (anti-relocation check). Counters (inversions, zero-area drops, fallback activations) become standing zero-gates from Stage 3 onward. CONTINUOUS MEASUREMENT: per-PR headless CPU evalSurface goal-vector gates (cutover Task 3.1 pattern) from Stage 0/1; nightly full matrix on the pinned real-GPU machine; two-tier hashing (machine-independent CPU (u,t)-topology hash; float-position hashes per GPU/driver pin) with determinism measured by duplicate runs before any CI gate is trusted, and the gate's red path demonstrated by injecting a known-bad commit. The final DoD is the Stage-6 matrix itself, green twice consecutively â€” the deliverable to the cutover plan is the measured all-zeros (or certified-exception) artifact, not a claim.
